title: sprint.py production close #1: autonomously implement and close one real ticket end-to-end
current_state: scripts/sprint.py is feature-complete - SAFE_COMPONENTS gate at line ~125 (scripts/, testing/, docs/, vault/), T-154 hypothesis/verified confidence gate at lines ~194-242, 15-min/30-iteration caps, $0.50 per-ticket budget, Telegram escalation on failure, commits only to sprint/T-NNN branches - but it has NEVER closed a ticket in production; only --dry-run has ever executed. ABOUT.md honestly marks it as Partial (T-169). Audit verdict: nothing structural needs loosening. The input pipeline (tickets/open/) was simply empty, and self-filed tickets defaulting to hypothesis confidence correctly block the auto path. Explicit --ticket selection is the by-design bypass (CLI flags verified at lines ~779-787).
target_state: sprint.py has autonomously planned, implemented, verified, and closed at least one real ticket end-to-end on a sprint/T-NNN branch: run transcript in logs/sprint/, verify PASS inside the run, SOLUTIONS.jsonl entry, ticket moved to closed/, Ash-reviewed diff. ABOUT.md's sprint-runner row flips from Partial to Done, honestly. This is the Track-2 flagship claim made real.
migration_plan: 1. Pick 1-2 small tickets with root_cause_confidence='verified' whose component sits entirely under SAFE_COMPONENTS. Best candidates from this batch: T-261 (testing/ + docs/), a T-252 promotion in scripts/passive/, or the T-253 checker fix if it turned out to be a small scripts/passive/ change. Each must carry a reproducing test.
2. Rehearse: python scripts/sprint.py --dry-run --ticket T-xxx; read the plan it produces in logs/sprint/.
4. Ash reviews the diff on the sprint/T-xxx branch and gates the merge (never auto-merge to main).
5. On success, update ABOUT.md sprint-runner status Partial -> Done and note the first production close date.
6. Do NOT widen SAFE_COMPONENTS this cycle - that is T-263's job, one prefix at a time.
risk_notes:
Failure modes: (a) wrong-fix-with-green-verify - mitigated by only running tickets that carry a reproducing test; (b) 15-min/30-iteration exhaustion - pick the smallest ticket available; (c) cost - capped at $0.50 by flag. If the run fails, the transcript in logs/sprint/ is itself the deliverable: file the diagnosis as a follow-up ticket rather than force-widening guardrails.

2026-07-06 status: blocked, not abandoned. Every other ticket in this hackathon-prep batch (T-251..T-271, T-273..T-275) was implemented by hand this session rather than left for the runner, which means the candidate pool this ticket needs (a small, verified, SAFE-component ticket) is currently empty - there is nothing genuine left in tickets/open/ for sprint.py to prove itself on. Ash's call (asked directly): skip for now rather than manufacture an artificial ticket. Revisit once a real small bug/ticket exists in the queue again - do not force this by inventing busywork.

2026-07-07: UNBLOCKED. The fresh-eyes audit found a genuine candidate and deliberately left it unfixed: T-276 (six dormant Windows-encoding landmines in testing/, root_cause_confidence=verified, testing/-only component, reproducing test specified). Run this ticket via sprint.py against T-276 per its migration_plan.
